name: Layered Toasted Hazelnut Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Spread hazelnuts in a single layer on a baking sheet and bake for 10-12 minutes until toasted and skins begin to flake off; cool slightly.
3. Wrap cooled hazelnuts in a heavy kitchen towel and rub against the towel to remove as much of the skins as possible.
4. Line an 8-inch square baking pan with foil, leaving a 1-inch overhang on the sides.
5. Lightly butter the foil; set aside.
6. Place hazelnuts into a food processor and process using ON/OFF pulsing action until coarsely chopped. Remove 1/2 cup and set aside; leave the rest in the food processor.
7. Continue processing until mixture looks like smooth peanut butter; set aside.
8. Spray or butter the inside of a heavy 4-quart saucepan with nonstick cooking spray.
9. Combine sugars, evaporated milk, and butter in the saucepan; bring to a full rolling boil over medium heat, stirring frequently to prevent scorching.
10. Attach a candy thermometer to the side of the pan and continue boiling until sugar mixture reaches soft-ball stage (238°F) on the candy thermometer, stirring constantly.
11. Reduce heat to low; stir in marshmallow creme, vanilla, and salt until blended.
12. Transfer 2 cups of the sugar mixture to a bowl and stir in the reserved hazelnut paste.
13. To the remaining mixture, add chocolate (mixture still in the pan); stir until blended. Stir in chopped hazelnuts.
14. Pour chocolate mixture into prepared pan and smooth evenly with a metal spatula or knife.
15. Pour reserved hazelnut (blonde) mixture on top of chocolate mixture and smooth top evenly with clean metal spatula.
16. Let candy sit for a minute, then score into 36 squares by cutting 6 sections lengthwise and 6 sections crosswise halfway through the fudge with a sharp knife.
17. Cool completely.
18. Remove cooled candy from foil by pulling on it; place on a cutting board.
19. Cut along score lines into squares and remove foil.
20. Makes 36 pieces. Store in an airtight container at room temperature.
